mmh.name: MMH
mmh.description: A private and calm macOS app for protecting personal photos, videos and local files.
homeHighlights: Private vault for personal photos and videos
Two-chip downloads for Apple Silicon and Intel Macs

metadata.networkAccess: No cloud sync or account required

mmh.installNote: After download, unzip the archive, drag MMH.app to Applications, then open from Launchpad. On first launch, macOS may ask you to allow the app in Privacy & Security settings.
features: Local-only encryption for photos and videos
No cloud sync — your data stays on device
Minimal UI designed for daily calm use

faq.question: Which download should I choose?
faq.answer: Apple Silicon (M1/M2/M3/M4) or Intel — check About This Mac → Chip / Processor. Download the matching .zip, unzip, and open MMH.app.

faq.question: Does MMH upload my files?
faq.answer: No. MMH is designed as a local-first privacy tool. Your media never leaves your Mac.

faq.question: Which macOS versions are supported?
faq.answer: MMH targets macOS 14 Sonoma and later.

screenshots.caption: Unlock your vault with the master password

filenest.name: FileNest
filenest.tagline: Collect, tag, and open your favorite folders on macOS
filenest.description: A lightweight macOS tool to manage and quickly access frequently used folders. Drag folders in, add tags and favorites, search, and open them in Finder from the app or menu bar.
homeHighlights: Fast access to the folders you use every day
Search, tags and favorites without a cloud account
Menu bar access plus JSON backup import/export

metadata.dataLocation: Local JSON library on your Mac
metadata.networkAccess: No login or cloud sync required

filenest.installNote: After download, unzip the archive (or use the DMG), drag FileNest.app to Applications, then open from Launchpad. On first launch, macOS may ask you to allow the app in Privacy & Security settings.
features: Drag folders in and organize with custom tags
Favorites and fast search across your library
Open any entry in Finder with one click
Menu bar quick access and recent folders
Import and export JSON backups — data stays local
No account, no cloud sync required

faq.question: Which download should I choose?
faq.answer: Apple Silicon (M1/M2/M3/M4) or Intel — check About This Mac → Chip / Processor. Download the matching .zip, unzip, and open FileNest.app.

faq.question: Where is my data stored?
faq.answer: All folders, tags, and settings are saved locally on your Mac as JSON. FileNest does not require login and does not sync to the cloud.

faq.question: How is FileNest different from Finder?
faq.answer: Finder is great for browsing any path. FileNest keeps the folders you care about in one place — tagged, searchable, and reachable from the window or menu bar.

faq.question: Which macOS versions are supported?
faq.answer: FileNest targets macOS 13 Ventura and later.

screenshots.caption: Edit folder — tags, favorites, and custom display name

filenest.ctaDescription: Email us for feedback, bugs, or feature ideas.

about.title: About
about.description: A personal software lab focused on privacy, productivity and thoughtful workflows.
about.body1: {name} is an independent product site for macOS utilities, productivity apps, and custom workflow systems.
about.body2: Current projects include MMH, FileNest, and the internal LC App workflow tool. They share one direction: quiet interfaces, local data when it matters, and features shaped around real work.
about.body3: This site ({host}) is the home for releases, documentation, changelogs, and writing from the lab.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.overview: {name} builds software with a local-first and privacy-respecting mindset. This policy describes data practices for our website and apps.
privacy.website: This marketing site may use basic analytics in the future. We do not sell personal data.
privacy.apps: Apps such as MMH process data on your device. Cloud features, if any, will be opt-in and documented per app.

terms.title: Terms of Service
terms.accept: By using {name} websites or software, you agree to these terms.
terms.license: Each app is provided under its own license. Beta builds are provided as-is without warranty.
terms.limit: We are not liable for indirect damages arising from use of our software.
